title: Introduction to Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS)

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign-in button
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.

tasks.title: Task 2: Create a Key Pair for the EC2 instances, inside the ECS Cluster

steps.text: Make sure you are in the N.Virginia Region. Navigate to EC2 Service by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on EC2 Service in the Compute section.
In the left navigation pane (scroll down) within Network & Security, click on the Key pairs
To create a new key pair, click on the Create key pair button.
Fill in the details below: Name: Enter WhizKeyPair Key pair type : RSA File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users) Leave other options as default. Click on the Create key pair button.
Fill in the details below:
Name: Enter WhizKeyPair
Key pair type : RSA
File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users)
Leave other options as default.
Click on the Create key pair button.
Task 2: Create a Key Pair for the EC2 instances, inside the ECS Cluster
Make sure you are in the N.Virginia Region. Navigate to Elastic Container Service by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on Elastic Container Service in the Containers section.
On the left sidebar, click on the Clusters option present under the Amazon ECS section, then Click on the Create cluster button.
Task 3: Launching an ECS Cluster
In Cluster configuration :
Cluster name: enter whizcluster

In Infrastructure Section : Select Fargate and Self-managed instances. Task 3: Launching an ECS Cluster

In this task, we are going to create a task definition for the cluster. A task definition provides a blueprint for creating tasks, which are the basic unit of work in ECS. Each task definition can define one or more containers that are run together on the same underlying EC2 instances or Fargate tasks.
On the left sidebar, click on the Task Definitions option present under the Amazon ECS section.
Click on the Create new task definition button.
Task 4: Create Task Definitions
In Task definition configuration:
Task definition family: Enter ecs-demo
Task 4: Create Task Definitions
For Infrastructure requirements : Launch Type : select Amazon EC2 InstancesTask size :CPU : Enter .25 vCPUMemory : Enter .5 GBIn Task Role: Choose noneIn task execution role: Choose none
For Infrastructure requirements :
Launch Type : select Amazon EC2 Instances
Task size :CPU : Enter .25 vCPUMemory : Enter .5 GBIn Task Role: Choose noneIn task execution role: Choose none
Task size :
CPU : Enter .25 vCPU
Memory : Enter .5 GBIn Task Role: Choose noneIn task execution role: Choose none
Memory : Enter .5 GB
In Task Role: Choose none
In task execution role: Choose none
Task 4: Create Task Definitions
Under Container - 1: Name: Enter httpd Image URL: Enter httpd:2.4
Under Container - 1:
Name: Enter httpd
Image URL: Enter httpd:2.4
Under Port mappings : Container port : Enter 80 Protocol : Select TCP App protocol : Select HTTP
Under Port mappings :
Container port : Enter 80
Protocol : Select TCP

Task 4: Create Task Definitions
Keep rest things as default and click on the Create button
Task Definition ecs-demo is now created.

tasks.title: Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS

steps.text: In this task, we are going to create a service and start the HTTPD container. In AWS ECS (Elastic Container Service), a service is a long-running task that ensures that a specified number of instances of a task definition are running and maintained in an ECS cluster. Services allow you to define the desired state of your tasks and automatically handle task placement, scaling, and recovery.
On the left sidebar, click on the Task definitions option present under the Amazon ECS section.
Select ecs-demo and click on Deploy and click Create service button.
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS
In Service details: Choose Task definition revision: select the latest revision.
Service name: Enter httpd
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS
Cluster will get selected by default.
In Compute configuration:
Choose Compute option as Launch type and select the Launch type as EC2
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS
In Deployment configuration, Scheduling strategy: Select REPLICA.
Desired tasks : Enter 1
In Networking section:
VPC: select the default vpc
Subnets: leave it as default
Security Group: click on Use an existing security group and select MySG.
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS
Keep other options as default, and click on the Create button.
The service will be created, wait till the Deployments and tasks changes to 1/1 tasks running
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS
Now navigate to Infrastructure tab, and scroll down to the Container Instances
Click on the EC2 Instance ID, and you will be redirected to the running EC2 instance.
Task 5: Create a service and start HTTPD container in ECS

tasks.title: Task 6: SSH into the underlying EC2 instance and run Docker commands

steps.text: Please follow the steps in SSH into EC2 Instance
Please note that the Ec2 instance connect will not work for the ECS instance, so please try other SSH methods.
Get the root access using the following command:sudo su
Get the root access using the following command:
Now run the updates using the following command: sudo dnf update -y
Now run the updates using the following command:
Check the Docker version by running the following command:docker version
Check the Docker version by running the following command:
Check all the docker processes running in the ECS Clusterdocker ps
Check all the docker processes running in the ECS Cluster
Default ECS agent and httpd container is running in the underlying EC2 instance
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
